response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack engineer with a long track record in TypeScript, Node.js, and web-oriented systems, but he does not meet the core technical bar for this role. The position requires deep, production-grade C++ or Rust expertise for systems-level routing data pipelines; his C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief role at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a single academic project. While GISCollective and Green Map demonstrate genuine geospatial interest, they are web-centric GIS platforms rather than high-performance routing or tile-generation infrastructure. There is no evidence of navigation guidance systems, routing tile pipelines, or geospatial data processing at the scale and latency requirements described.
strengths: Geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective (event-driven geo-data platform) and multiple Green Map GIS applications
Demonstrated low-latency, high-throughput systems thinking at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O and caching
Broad distributed-systems and DevOps experience including Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD, and microservices architecture
concerns: C++ expertise is superficial and over a decade old; Rust is entirely absent from the resume and skills list
No experience with routing tile generation, navigation guidance, or map data pipeline engineering
Primary career trajectory is firmly in web and product engineering (TypeScript/Node.js), not systems programming or geospatial data infrastructure
